prompt: Repair bin/homedir-quota. The command is invoked as `bin/homedir-quota DATA_DIR ACCOUNT REQUESTED_GIB`; DATA_DIR contains the TSV fixtures documented by their header rows. A request is eligible only when it is an increase and current usage is at least 80% of the current limit. Its effective ceiling is the minimum of the account's approval ceiling, the filesystem capacity ceiling (`current limit + filesystem quota capacity - all current limits on that filesystem`), and its previous-exception ceiling when one exists. An eligible request must either set the target account's quota to the exact requested GiB value or be denied without changing quotas.tsv. Never silently reduce or partially apply an over-ceiling request. Keep the stable evidence output and exit-code contract (0 applied, 2 policy denial, 64 invalid input), preserve every unrelated account, and use only the supplied fixtures; do not call host quota or filesystem utilities. Do not modify fixtures or protected tests.
